documentation_links.title: Performing Safe Canary Upgrades with Dry Run

recommendations.problem: Runtime bug in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0 prevents proper baseline management

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Check "Configuration" tab for Runtime version

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics get-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region>
solution.description: Verify the canary is using syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Dry run"
# 4. Select syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later as the runtime version
# 5. Review dry run results before applying to production

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics start-canary-dry-run \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0
solution.description: Use dry run to safely test syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 before production upgrade
solution.expected_outcome: Dry run completes successfully, confirming compatibility with version 11.0
solution.step: Test upgrade using dry run feature

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Edit"
# 4. Update runtime version to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later
# 5. Save changes

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics update-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0
solution.description: Upgrade runtime to version with the baseline fix
solution.expected_outcome: Canary updated to new runtime version

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click on a recent run to view details
# 4. Get the "baseCanaryRunId" from the log details
# 5. Run canary again and verify the baseCanaryRunId remains unchanged
solution.description: Confirm baseline is set once and respected in subsequent runs by checking S3 artifacts
solution.expected_outcome: Baseline artifacts in S3 remain stable, not updated on every run

recommendations.title: Upgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later

root_cause: A bug in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0's VisualValidator fails to detect an existing visualReference.json in S3 when BaseCanaryRunId is set to "nextrun". The runtime incorrectly evaluates the baseline as absent on every run, overwrites visualReference.json with the current run's screenshots, and never performs an actual visual comparison. Fixed in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0.
symptoms: Baseline screenshot is overwritten on every canary run
Visual monitoring baseline not being respected
BaseCanaryRunId set to 'nextrun' but baseline updates every execution
Existing baseline in S3 not recognized by runtime
Each run treated as new baseline instead of comparison
title: Visual monitoring baseline overwritten every run with BaseCanaryRunId 'nextrun'
